description: You are a meteorologist at a National Weather Service office configuring GPredict for polar-orbiting weather satellite tracking. Update the PolarWX module by removing the ISS and adding the following polar-orbiting weather satellites: SUOMI NPP (NORAD 37849), FENGYUN 3A (NORAD 32958), FENGYUN 3B (NORAD 37214), and DMSP 5D-3 F18 / USA 210 (NORAD 35951). Add ground stations for the Fairbanks, AK receive site (64.8378 N, 147.7164 W, 133m) and the Anchorage, AK receive site (61.2181 N, 149.9003 W, 38m). Ensure GPredict is configured to use metric units (kilometers) for all distance and speed measurements. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm a satellite meteorologist at the National Weather Service and I need to fix our GPredict configuration. The PolarWX tracking module has the wrong satellites in it — it's tracking ISS instead of our actual polar weather satellite constellation. Please remove ISS from PolarWX and add these four operational polar-orbiting weather satellites: SUOMI NPP (NORAD 37849), FENGYUN 3A (NORAD 32958), FENGYUN 3B (NORAD 37214), and DMSP 5D-3 F18 which is also known as USA 210 (NORAD 35951). I also need you to add two Alaska ground stations: Fairbanks at 64.8378 North, 147.7164 West, 133 meters altitude; and Anchorage at 61.2181 North, 149.9003 West, 38 meters altitude. Finally, please set GPredict to use metric units in the preferences.
